label: SES-058 Audit feature v1.2 workstream established: 4 design decisions captured (DEC-171..174), audit-v1.2-planning.md authored, decomposed into 11-prompt Claude Code series (audit-v1.2 A..K); 1 session, 4 decisions, 11 planning items, 15 references

session.title: Audit feature v1.2 workstream established: planning conversation captured four design decisions, authored audit-v1.2-planning.md, decomposed into 11-prompt Claude Code series
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai planning conversation. Opened on Doug's seed prompt asking to add a CRM-reverse-engineering capability to the V1 tool. Initial investigation surfaced that the requested feature already shipped as the v1.1 Audit feature on 2026-04-14 (commit 1eabfbb), with the audit_manager (936 lines), audit_db (487 lines), audit_utils (319 lines), audit_worker (183 lines), audit_entry UI (521 lines), and a 610-line feature PRD marked Implemented. Doug clarified that he wanted to extend the v1.1 feature with three additions: entity-level selection (avoid all-or-nothing category scope); audit support for filtered tabs (filtered_tab_manager.py is shipped on the deploy side but the audit doesn't yet capture them); and audit support for users, teams, and roles. The conversation then surfaced four genuinely consequential decisions in sequence under ARCHITECTURE mode using the eight-element decision template, plus one routine decision decided as a draft, and produced the audit-v1.2-planning.md document committed in this sandbox (commit ef9799a) as the design authority for the implementation work to follow.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "I would like to add the ability for the V1 tool to connect to a CRM, and reverse engineer yaml files from selected entities or system information."

Structured summary of the planning conversation:

(1) Codebase investigation revealed the feature already exists. The v1.1 Audit feature shipped on 2026-04-14 (commit 1eabfbb) with full orchestrator, database insertion, classification utilities, worker, sidebar UI, and feature PRD at PRDs/product/features/feat-audit.md marked Status: Implemented. AuditOptions currently exposes six category-level toggles (include_custom_fields, include_native_custom_fields, include_detail_layouts, include_list_layouts, include_relationships, include_native_fields) but no per-entity scope filter. The four metadata-discovery API methods exist on api_client.py: get_all_scopes, get_entity_full_metadata, get_all_links, get_entity_field_list. The InstanceRole enum (source/target/both) gates entry pickers per role.

(2) Three gaps identified for v1.2. Gap one: entity-level scope (no way to audit only the entities that changed). Gap two: filtered tabs (filtered_tab_manager.py exists at 635 lines deploying both halves of the Report Filter plus custom-scope metadata pattern, but audit_manager has no _discover_filtered_tabs). Gap three: security structure (schema v1.3 Section 12 specifies roles, teams, scope access, system permissions, role-aware visibility in over 500 lines, but Section 12 is paper-only — config_loader.py does not reference roles or teams at all, models.py has no RoleDefinition or TeamDefinition dataclass, and no role_manager or team_manager exists in espo_impl/core).

(3) Decision 1 — Users out of audit scope. Three options framed: A (users captured to non-YAML side artifact like users.json), B (schema bump to include user-to-role assignment in YAML), C (users out of scope). Recommended Option C on the rationale that the audit's identity is a YAML-round-trip tool, Section 12 explicitly excludes user assignment from the deployment artifact, and adding non-YAML side output would shift the feature from configuration-capture to configuration-plus-data-snapshot without current operational driver. Doug confirmed Option C: "for now, we are interested in recreating the structure not the user data."

(4) Decision 2 — Roles/teams audit-only versus audit-plus-deploy. Four options framed: A (audit emits, Configure silently no-ops on unknown top-level keys), A-prime (audit emits, Configure treats as NOT_SUPPORTED like saved views/duplicate checks/workflows), B (audit emits plus add role_manager and team_manager deploy-side in same workstream), C (defer security entirely). Initial recommendation was A-prime (matches existing project convention for the three other NOT_SUPPORTED features). Doug overrode toward Option B: "I want to resolve both sides of the issue, so option B."

(5) Decision 2.5 — Scope of Section 12 in the workstream. With Option B committed, the sub-decision was how much of Section 12 to do. Three options: A (all five parts including 12.5 role-aware visibility), B (12.1 through 12.4 plus 12.6, defer 12.5 which requires extending condition_expression.py with a new role: leaf clause variant and threading role predicates through dynamic-logic validators), C (12.1 plus 12.2 only — bare role/team identity). Recommended Option B on the rationale that 12.5 is structurally different work (parser extension versus REST CHECK→ACT) with no current operational driver (zero CBM YAML uses role clauses today). Doug chose Option A: full Section 12 in this workstream.

(6) Decision 3 — Entity picker UX (decided as routine draft, did not pass the two-part test). Pre-flight live discovery versus cached entity list. Pre-flight wins: a single get_all_scopes call adds 1-2 seconds to dialog open; the cached alternative requires client DB schema, invalidation logic, and a manual-refresh UX for no real benefit since a full audit also takes only minutes. Routine choice; recorded as DEC-174 anyway because it is a definite architectural commitment.

(7) Workstream design and series outline. With four decisions settled, decomposed the workstream into 11 Claude Code prompts named audit-v1.2-A through audit-v1.2-K. The series tag mirrors the existing yaml-v1.1 eight-prompt convention. Prompt A: roles/teams top-level recognition plus raw-passthrough dataclasses (loader/models only, no deploy). Prompt B: structured scope_access and system_permissions parsing (Section 12.3 and 12.4). Prompt C: team_manager.py deploy-side CHECK→ACT. Prompt D: role_manager.py deploy-side including scope_access to EspoCRM permission translation — most substantive new module. Prompt E: Section 12.6 deploy ordering plus security pipeline step in run_worker.py. Prompt F: condition_expression.py extension with role: leaf clause variant. Prompt G: Section 12.5 wiring — role-aware requiredWhen/visibleWhen, role-scoped layouts. Prompt H: audit-side _discover_roles, _discover_teams, role-aware logic reversal, security.yaml emission. Prompt I: filtered-tab audit capture. Prompt J: entity-picker UI plus AuditOptions.selected_entities with pre-flight discovery. Prompt K: feat-audit.md bumped to v1.2 plus user-guide updates.

(8) Planning document authored. PRDs/product/crmbuilder-automation-PRD/audit-v1.2-planning.md (commit ef9799a) is the design authority for the 11-prompt series. Sections: Motivation, Current State Survey (against commit 7e4592f), Design Decisions Captured (Decisions 1, 2, 2.5, 3 with rationale and consequences), Workstream Design End-to-End, Prompt Series Plan with per-prompt dependencies and validation criteria, End State, Out of Scope (including Section 12.7 field-level permissions, user records, general topological deploy ordering, the cross-batch validator-server-state issue, NOT_SUPPORTED resurrection, and v1.1 schema audit coverage extensions), Known Interactions and Risks, and four Open Questions (persona cross-reference validation, empty scope_access warning, defaults for new AuditOptions flags, v1.1-to-v1.2 re-audit overwrite behavior).

(9) Doug approved the planning document as-is, including the provisional answers to the four open questions: persona cross-reference validation = no for v1.2; empty scope_access = warn in audit log only; new AuditOptions flag defaults = False; v1.1-to-v1.2 re-audit = overwrite.

(10) Conversation flow decision. With the planning artifact complete and approved, two natural next moves were flagged: switch to DETAIL mode and write Prompt A in this same session, or close out first and write Prompt A in a fresh conversation. Recommended close-out-first on the rationale that Prompt A authoring is substantial enough to deserve a fresh conversation with full context budget and the close-out is cleaner-scoped to the planning phase alone. Doug confirmed: close out now, Prompt A goes to a fresh conversation.
session.artifacts_produced: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-automation-PRD/audit-v1.2-planning.md (commit ef9799a, this conversation); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_058.json (this payload); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-058.md (apply prompt); four decisions DEC-171 DEC-172 DEC-173 DEC-174; eleven planning items PI-034 through PI-044; fifteen references (four decided_in linking each decision to SES-058, eleven is_about linking SES-058 to each of the eleven new planning items).
session.in_flight_at_end: Audit feature v1.2 workstream queued for execution as eleven sequential Claude Code prompts (audit-v1.2 A through K) in dependency order. The next conversation opens against the audit-v1.2-planning.md document at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-automation-PRD/audit-v1.2-planning.md and produces CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-A-roles-teams-recognition.md (PI-034) as its first deliverable. After Doug runs Prompt A through Claude Code locally and confirms green, the next conversation writes Prompt B, and so on. The planning document is the design authority for the full series; individual prompts must remain consistent with it or amend it via UPDATE-PROMPT. End state at workstream completion (PI-044 resolved): the CRM Audit feature is at v1.2 with per-entity selection, filtered-tab round-trip, and full v1.3 Section 12 security round-trip including role-aware visibility. Out-of-scope items deferred per the planning doc: Section 12.7 field-level permissions, user records audit, general topological deploy ordering for non-security cross-file dependencies, validator-server-state for cross-batch field references, NOT_SUPPORTED feature resurrection (saved views, duplicate checks, workflows), and audit support for v1.1 schema additions beyond filtered tabs (entity settings, email templates, formula fields).

decisions.title: User records are out of scope for the v1.2 audit feature
decisions.context: Schema v1.3 Section 12 explicitly states that user-to-role assignment is out of YAML scope and that the deployment artifact describes the security structure (what each role can do), not who is assigned to what. The v1.1 audit feature does not capture user records today. The Decision 1 fork in the audit-v1.2 planning conversation was whether to extend the audit to also capture user records as a side artifact (Option A — non-YAML users.json), to push for a schema bump bringing user-to-role assignment into the deployment artifact (Option B), or to leave user records out of scope entirely (Option C).
decisions.decision: Option C — user records remain out of scope for the v1.2 audit feature. The audit captures only what the v1.3 schema can represent. No new GET /User method on api_client.py. No users.json artifact. No user records inserted into the client database. If user-roster visibility becomes operationally important later, it can be a separate feature (working name: CRM Instance Inspector) without disrupting the audit.
decisions.rationale: Doug's stated rationale: "for now, we are interested in recreating the structure not the user data." The audit's product identity is a YAML-round-trip tool; the output is something an operator can edit and reapply against a target instance. Adding a non-YAML side artifact for users would shift the feature from configuration-capture to configuration-plus-data-snapshot — a meaningfully different product with no current operational driver. The Section 12 design decision to exclude user assignment from YAML is recent (v1.3) and intentional, motivated by the observation that user assignment changes far more frequently than security structure and is appropriately managed in the target CRM admin UI.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option A — users captured to a non-YAML side artifact like users.json alongside the YAML files, paginated GET /User over 200-record batches. Rejected because it changes the audit's product identity and there is no current operational pressure for user-roster visibility. Option B — schema bump to bring user-to-role assignment into the deployment artifact. Rejected because reversing the Section 12 design decision is a much bigger discussion than this audit feature and would conflict with the design principle that user assignment changes do not require redeploy.
decisions.consequences: The v1.2 audit captures security structure (roles, teams, scope access, system permissions, role-aware visibility per DEC-172 and DEC-173) but not user records. Operators recreate user assignments manually in each target instance after deploying audited security YAML. If a future feature needs user-roster visibility (for example, comparing source-instance user roster against target-instance user roster after a deployment), it becomes its own feature outside the audit pipeline.

decisions.title: Roles and teams are audited and deployed in the same audit-v1.2 workstream
decisions.context: Schema v1.3 Section 12 specifies roles, teams, scope access, system permissions, and role-aware visibility in over 500 lines. The schema is paper-only: config_loader.py does not reference roles or teams, models.py has no RoleDefinition or TeamDefinition dataclass, and no role_manager.py or team_manager.py exists in espo_impl/core/. Auditing roles and teams from a source instance is technically straightforward (read endpoints exist on EspoCRM's Role and Team entities) but produces YAML that the deploy engine cannot apply. The Decision 2 fork was how to handle this asymmetry between audit-side capability and deploy-side capability.
decisions.decision: Option B — roles and teams are both audited AND deployed in the same audit-v1.2 workstream. This workstream adds the deploy-side role_manager.py and team_manager.py modules following the existing CHECK→ACT pattern, plus the audit-side _discover_roles and _discover_teams methods, plus all the schema plumbing (loader recognition, model dataclasses, validator extensions, pipeline orchestration). The end state is full round-trip on security structure within a single workstream.
decisions.rationale: Doug's stated rationale: "I want to resolve both sides of the issue, so option B." Bundling deploy-side managers into the audit workstream produces a coherent round-trip-clean feature at workstream end. The alternative of shipping audit-only and treating roles/teams as NOT_SUPPORTED (Option A-prime, matching how saved views, duplicate checks, and workflows are handled today) would leave the user with half-deployable security YAML and require re-opening the workstream later when the deploy-side managers eventually land.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option A — audit emits, Configure silently no-ops on unknown top-level keys. Rejected as misleading; the operator would think security was deployed when it was not. Option A-prime — audit emits, Configure treats as NOT_SUPPORTED with a clear MANUAL CONFIGURATION REQUIRED block at the end of the run, matching the existing three NOT_SUPPORTED features (saved views, duplicate checks, workflows). This was the original recommendation, but Doug elected to resolve both sides. Option C — defer security entirely (audit captures only entities/fields/layouts/relationships/filtered tabs; security is its own future workstream covering both audit and deploy together). Rejected because the operational pressure for security round-trip exists now and bundling is straightforward.
decisions.consequences: The audit-v1.2 workstream expands from approximately five to six prompts (entity selection, filtered-tab audit, schema plumbing) to eleven prompts (adding deploy-side managers, condition_expression role clause, layout/panel role scoping, and audit-side reversal of role-aware dynamic logic). At workstream end, security structure round-trips cleanly between source and target instances. Personal user-to-role assignment remains a manual step in the target CRM admin UI per DEC-171.

decisions.title: All five parts of schema v1.3 Section 12 are in scope for the audit-v1.2 workstream, including role-aware visibility (Section 12.5)
decisions.context: Schema v1.3 Section 12 has five sub-sections: 12.1 Roles, 12.2 Teams, 12.3 Scope Access, 12.4 System Permissions, 12.5 Role-Aware Visibility, plus 12.6 Deploy Ordering. Sections 12.1 through 12.4 are CHECK→ACT REST operations against EspoCRM's Role and Team entities — the same architectural pattern as every other deploy-side manager in espo_impl/core/. Section 12.5 is qualitatively different work: it extends condition_expression.py with a new role: leaf clause variant, threads role predicates through the existing dynamic-logic validators, adds role-resolution to the audit's reverse-engineering of EspoCRM's logic clauses, and reaches into field-level requiredWhen/visibleWhen, panel visibility, and layouts. The Decision 2.5 fork was which parts of Section 12 to include in the workstream.
decisions.decision: Option A — all five parts of Section 12 (12.1 Roles, 12.2 Teams, 12.3 Scope Access, 12.4 System Permissions, 12.5 Role-Aware Visibility) plus 12.6 Deploy Ordering are in scope for the audit-v1.2 workstream. Section 12.7 (field-level permissions and named permission presets) remains explicitly deferred per the schema doc.
decisions.rationale: Doug elected to resolve security comprehensively in one workstream rather than stage 12.5 to a follow-up. Bundling 12.5 now avoids re-opening the security workstream later and produces complete v1.3 Section 12 coverage in a single shot. The known disadvantage — 12.5 is structurally different work requiring parser extension — is accepted in exchange for closure.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option B — 12.1 through 12.4 plus 12.6 in this workstream, defer 12.5. Recommended originally because 12.5 is structurally different work and zero CBM YAML uses role clauses today (no current operational driver), but rejected by Doug in favor of comprehensive closure. Option C — 12.1 plus 12.2 only (bare role/team identity, no scope_access or system_permissions or role-aware visibility). Rejected because it would capture who the roles are without capturing what they can do, eliminating most of the value of Section 12.
decisions.consequences: The audit-v1.2 workstream grows by approximately four prompts (specifically Prompts F and G) to cover 12.5. condition_expression.py gains a new RoleClause AST variant and the parser/validator/render functions thread it through. Validators check that role names referenced in clauses exist in the program batch's roles: block. The audit's _reverse_dynamic_logic method picks up role-aware clauses from EspoCRM's clientDefs/entityDefs dynamic-logic JSON shape and emits them in the YAML output. Layout-level forRoles: scoping (Section 12.5.2) is implemented as a layout-set selector at deploy time. The evaluate_condition function is not extended for role clauses because role-aware evaluation requires current-user context that the deploy engine does not have — that boundary is correct and not a regression. End state at workstream completion: full v1.3 Section 12 (excluding 12.7 per schema doc) is round-trip-deployable.

decisions.title: The audit dialog entity picker uses pre-flight live discovery rather than a cached entity list
decisions.context: The audit-v1.2 workstream adds entity-level scope selection (new AuditOptions.selected_entities field plus an entity-picker UI in audit_entry.py). The picker needs to be populated from somewhere. Two viable approaches: pre-flight live discovery (when the user opens the dialog, call get_all_scopes against the source instance and show the live result) or cached entity list (persist the previous audit's entity list in the client SQLite database, show it immediately on dialog open, refresh on demand).
decisions.decision: Pre-flight live discovery. When the user opens the Audit dialog, it calls client.get_all_scopes synchronously (with a brief loading state in the dialog) and populates the entity picker QListWidget with checkboxes from the live result, defaulting all checked. Adds approximately one to two seconds to dialog open time.
decisions.rationale: Decided as a routine ARCHITECTURE-mode draft — did not pass the two-part test because both options produce the same end state and the difference is a UX speed-versus-staleness tradeoff. Pre-flight wins because the alternative requires client DB schema for the cache, invalidation logic, and a manual-refresh affordance — meaningful complexity for no real benefit. A full audit also takes only minutes; one additional API round trip on dialog open is operationally invisible. Recorded as DEC-174 anyway because it is a definite architectural commitment that informs Prompt J's implementation.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Cached entity list — persist the previous audit's entity list in the client database, show it immediately on dialog open, provide a manual Refresh button. Rejected because it requires new client DB schema, cache invalidation logic, and the Refresh UX affordance for no operationally meaningful benefit.
decisions.consequences: No new client DB schema for cached entity lists. Prompt J in the audit-v1.2 series implements the picker as a synchronous get_all_scopes call when the dialog opens. The picker fails gracefully (clear error message in the dialog, no crash) when the source instance is unreachable or returns an auth error. Default state: all entities checked, matching the v1.1 behavior of auditing all categories.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt A — roles and teams top-level recognition plus raw-passthrough dataclasses
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-A-roles-teams-recognition.md in PRDs/product/crmbuilder-automation-PRD/. The prompt adds: config_loader.py recognition of roles: and teams: as valid top-level keys; models.py RoleDefinition and TeamDefinition dataclasses with raw-passthrough fields for scope_access_raw and system_permissions_raw mirroring how saved_views_raw and workflows_raw work today; ProgramFile.roles list and ProgramFile.teams list collections; hard-reject for malformed structures (missing name:, wrong types). Does NOT add structured parsing of scope_access or system_permissions (Prompt B) or any deploy-side execution (Prompts C and D). Acceptance: a test YAML with valid roles and teams blocks loads cleanly; a test YAML with a role missing name: produces a clear validation error; all existing tests pass.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt B — structured scope_access and system_permissions parsing
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-B-scope-access-system-permissions.md. Adds: ScopeAccess dataclass per role.entity (Section 12.3) with entity_name, read, edit, delete, stream, assignmentPermission, recordAccessControl fields; SystemPermissions dataclass per role (Section 12.4) with the v1.3 enumeration of system-level permission keys; RoleDefinition.scope_access dict mapping entity_name to ScopeAccess; RoleDefinition.system_permissions SystemPermissions; validators for scope_access entity name resolution against batch context plus v1.3 permission value enumeration. Does NOT add the 12.5 role: leaf clause (Prompt F). Depends on PI-034. Acceptance: Section 12 examples from app-yaml-schema.md parse and roundtrip cleanly through the loader; invalid values produce specific error messages.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt C — team_manager.py deploy-side CHECK→ACT
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-C-team-manager.md. Adds: espo_impl/core/team_manager.py implementing CHECK→ACT against EspoCRM's Team entity; api_client.py gains get_teams, create_team, update_team methods; TeamStatus enum and TeamResult dataclass in models.py (OK, CREATED, UPDATED, ERROR). Depends on PI-034 (model and loader prerequisites). Acceptance: a YAML declaring a new team applies to a target instance; re-running is idempotent (CHECK passes, no spurious updates); modifying an existing team's description updates without creating a duplicate. Team deletion is out of scope per the existing managers' conservative-deletion convention; document this.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt D — role_manager.py deploy-side CHECK→ACT including scope_access translation
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-D-role-manager.md. The most substantive new deploy-side module in the series. Adds: espo_impl/core/role_manager.py implementing CHECK→ACT against EspoCRM's Role entity; api_client.py gains get_roles, create_role, update_role methods; RoleStatus enum and RoleResult dataclass in models.py; the scope_access translation layer mapping YAML scope_access blocks to EspoCRM Role record's data field shape (per-entity-per-action permission matrix with assignment scope); system_permissions translation mapping YAML system_permissions keys to EspoCRM Role record's top-level permission columns. Depends on PI-035 (structured scope_access parsing) and PI-036 (team_manager precedent). Acceptance: a test role with scope_access on three entities applies cleanly with correct per-entity permissions on the deployed Role record; re-running is idempotent; modifying a scope_access value redeploys correctly; referencing a non-existent entity in scope_access produces a clear pre-deploy error.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt E — Section 12.6 deploy ordering and security pipeline step
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-E-deploy-ordering.md. Adds: a new pipeline step Security in run_worker._run_full, ordered after Workflows per existing orchestration sequence; the step calls team_manager first (teams have no entity dependencies) then role_manager (roles reference entity names that must exist on the target); within-batch dependency resolution ensures security YAMLs are processed last regardless of filename alphabetical position; updated STEP SUMMARY block to include security step status. Resolves a known interaction: the existing backlog notes that the deploy engine processes files in alphabetical order rather than topological order. This prompt adds a security-specific special-case rule (security files always run last); it does NOT solve the general topological-sort backlog issue. Depends on PI-036 and PI-037. Acceptance: a batch containing entity YAMLs and a security.yaml deploys cleanly regardless of filename order; a security YAML referencing an entity not in the batch and not on the server produces a clear error before any API write.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt F — role: leaf clause variant in condition_expression.py
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-F-role-clause.md. Adds: condition_expression.py gains a new leaf clause variant for role: predicates (Section 12.5.1); AST extension with RoleClause joining the existing LeafClause, AllNode, AnyNode set; parse_condition recognizes role: keys at the leaf level; validate_condition accepts an optional known_roles set and validates role names against it if provided; render_condition emits role clauses in structured form. evaluate_condition is deliberately NOT extended for roles — role-aware evaluation requires current-user context that the deploy engine does not have; the boundary is correct, validators ensure clauses are well-formed and actual evaluation happens in the target CRM at runtime. Depends on PI-034 (RoleDefinition exists). Acceptance: round-trip of a YAML requiredWhen clause with a role predicate parses and renders correctly; compound clauses mixing role and record-state predicates parse correctly; invalid role names (when known_roles is provided) produce specific errors.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt G — Section 12.5 wiring: role-aware field/panel visibility and role-scoped layouts
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-G-role-aware-visibility.md. Adds: config_loader.py validates that requiredWhen and visibleWhen clauses with role: predicates reference roles declared in the program batch's roles: block; field_manager.py and layout_manager.py translate role-aware clauses into EspoCRM's dynamic-logic JSON when writing to the target; layout-level forRoles: scoping (Section 12.5.2) is implemented as a layout-set selector at deploy time; validator cross-checks ensure every role referenced in any condition expression exists in the batch's roles: block or on the target instance. Depends on PI-037 (role_manager) and PI-039 (role clause parser). Acceptance: a YAML with a field requiredWhen clause referencing the Mentor role deploys correctly with role-aware dynamic logic on the target; a layout with forRoles: [Mentor Administrator] deploys only to that role's effective layout; a field with a role clause referencing an undeclared role produces a clear pre-deploy error.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt H — audit-side _discover_roles, _discover_teams, role-aware logic reversal, security.yaml emission
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-H-audit-security.md. Adds: audit_manager.py _discover_roles and _discover_teams methods using new get_roles and get_teams api_client methods; RoleAuditResult and TeamAuditResult dataclasses; _reverse_dynamic_logic extended to recognize and emit role: leaf clauses when it sees EspoCRM's role-aware dynamic-logic JSON shape; _write_yaml_files writes a separate security.yaml containing roles: and teams: blocks when security was captured and any results exist; AuditOptions.include_security boolean (default False per the planning doc Open Question 9.3); audit_db.py inserts role and team records into new client-schema tables (Alembic-style migration). Depends on PI-034 through PI-040 (full schema and deploy work in place so audited output is round-trip-deployable). Acceptance: an audit against a source with three roles and two teams produces security.yaml with correct content; re-running deploy on the target reproduces the same role and team records; a source field with a role-aware requiredWhen clause is captured with the role predicate intact; audit with include_security=False produces no security.yaml and no role/team DB records.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt I — filtered-tab audit capture
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-I-filtered-tab-audit.md. Adds: audit_manager.py _discover_filtered_tabs method called after relationship discovery; FilteredTabAuditResult dataclass; reverse-engineering walks both halves of the pattern — for each audited entity, query list_report_filters(entity) for Report Filter records and read clientDefs.{entity} metadata to identify custom scope tabs binding to those filters; YAML emission slots into each entity's existing filteredTabs: block in the per-entity YAML; AuditOptions.include_filtered_tabs boolean (default False per planning doc Open Question 9.3); audit_db.py inserts filtered-tab records into a new client-schema table. Depends on PI-041 only for sequencing convenience — no logical dependency. Acceptance: an audit against a source with two filtered tabs (one per entity) produces YAML entity blocks each containing one correct filteredTabs entry; re-deploying the audited YAML against a fresh target with Advanced Pack present reproduces both filtered tabs; audit with include_filtered_tabs=False produces no records and no YAML blocks. Graceful failure: HTTP 404 from list_report_filters (Advanced Pack not installed) is treated as no filtered tabs to capture.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt J — entity-picker UI and AuditOptions.selected_entities
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-J-entity-picker.md. Adds: AuditOptions.selected_entities (set of str or None; None means all, preserving current v1.1 behavior); _discover_entities extended to filter to selected_entities after fetching scopes; audit_entry.py gains a pre-flight discovery step on dialog open calling client.get_all_scopes synchronously with a brief loading state and populating a scrollable entity-picker QListWidget with checkboxes (default all checked); Select All and Select None buttons above the picker; integration with existing category checkboxes (entity picker says which entities, category checkboxes say what aspects); new checkboxes Filtered tabs and Security (roles, teams, role-aware visibility). Implements DEC-174 (pre-flight live discovery, not cached). No logical dependency on PI-034 through PI-042 — purely UI work — but slotted late so the dialog can reference the new include_security and include_filtered_tabs flags. Acceptance: selecting two entities out of ten in the picker results in only those two being audited end-to-end; Select None disables the Run button; pre-flight discovery failure shows a clear error without crashing the dialog.

planning_items.title: audit-v1.2 Prompt K — feat-audit.md v1.2 and user-guide updates
planning_items.description: Author CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-audit-v1.2-K-documentation.md. Adds: PRDs/product/features/feat-audit.md bumped from v1.1 to v1.2 with new sections covering entity-level selection, filtered-tab audit, and full Section 12 security round-trip; docs/user/user-guide.md updated section on the Audit feature with new screenshots and option descriptions; updated entries in crmbuilder repo CLAUDE.md describing the new managers, dataclasses, and security pipeline step. Depends on PI-034 through PI-043 (all functional work complete so the documentation reflects what actually shipped, not what was planned). Acceptance: feat-audit.md v1.2 accurately describes the shipped feature read against the actual code; user-guide steps reproduce successfully against a test instance.
